mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and construction activity, driving higher corporate profit expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which immediately boosts market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.01 during early-cycle periods, or signal predictive power decays within 2 days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL momentum coincides with positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or predictive alpha vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting consumer income growth and driving positive equity market returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in walk-forward validation, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand growth and corporate revenue expansion, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day investment horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which forces equity valuations lower and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 50% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample tests.
